title: Comparatives: write the comparative form
lesson: To compare two things, short adjectives add -er (fast → faster) and longer ones use 'more' (more interesting). Spelling shifts: final -y becomes -ier (happy → happier), and a short vowel + single consonant doubles it (big → bigger). 'good' and 'bad' are irregular (better, worse).

items.text: This box is {1} than that one.
items.explanation: A one/two-syllable adjective ending in -y changes to -ier: heavy → heavier.

items.text: A train is {1} than a bus.
This book is {1} than the film.
items.explanation: Adjectives of three or more syllables use 'more': more interesting.
A short vowel + single consonant doubles the consonant: hot → hotter.
Double the final consonant after a short vowel: big → bigger.
